title: E-2 Case Study | From Sea Captain to Freight Owner
content:
## Freight transportation entrepreneur
Our Chilean client, an investor seeking to establish a refrigerated freight company in the United States , secur ed E- 2 Treaty Investor status after an intensive review process. Th is case, handled by Attorney Dallan Bunce , was approved in just 3 months and 16 days on October 9, 2025, with premium processing , despite facing strict timelines and a complex Request for Evidence (RFE) . Through focused coordination, careful documentation, and a proactive response strategy, Attorney Bunce helped the client move from a stressful waiting period to a successful approval , ensuring his business could move forward without interruption.

## From Sea Captain to Roadway Entrepreneur
Before entering the world of U.S. logistics, our client spent more than ten years commanding vessels in South America’s maritime industry. As a ship captain, he managed crews, coordinated transport operations, and ensured the safe passage of passengers and freight through challenging coastal routes. Those years at sea built his discipline, leadership, and problem-solving skills.
After moving to the United States, he transitioned into on-land logistics, gaining firsthand experience in freight movement across multiple states. His ability to adapt to new environments and uphold safety standards earned him a strong reputation among carriers. Alongside his professional work, he completed formal training in commercial driving and logistics management, equipping him with the skills to oversee operations both behind the wheel and from a managerial standpoint.
His story reflects determination and growth. What began on open waters evolved into a mission to build a transport company that would serve one of the country’s most demanding industries: temperature-controlled freight. His background in navigation and logistics became the foundation for a venture that combined precision, responsibility, and vision.
When the client reached out to Colombo & Hurd, he was close to losing his status in the United States. The urgency left little room for error or delay. Attorney Dallan Bunce recalled, “He was running out of time. If we had submitted even a little late, he would have been out of status.”
The goal was clear: to secure a change of status through an E-2 investment in a refrigerated freight company. Launching a business from the ground up while working against a fixed deadline, however, came with heavy pressure. Every document, signature, and transaction needed to fall into place quickly and precisely.
There was also a complication with the source of funds. As Attorney Bunce explained, “evidence he expected to use wasn’t as clear or illustrative after the second or third viewing as we thought it was.” What began as a simple investment turned into a detailed process of verifying and tracing every transfer. With little time left, the team had to prove that every dollar came directly from the client and was properly documented.
Get your free E-2 profile evaluation today.

## Precision, Persistence, and Speed
Faced with a tight deadline, Attorney Dallan Bunce and our legal team built their strategy around urgency and constant communication. Every step was designed to keep the case moving without pause. As he explained, “We approached everything with the filing deadline in mind.”
The first step was to help the client identify a business model that could be launched quickly and managed effectively. Once the investment direction was set, the team shifted their focus to documentation. Instead of waiting for full sets of files, they encouraged the client to send every piece of evidence the moment it became available.
“We told him not to wait until he had all the documents together,” Attorney Bunce said. “As soon as he got one, he sent it to us, and we looked at it right away.” This steady flow of updates allowed the team to review, correct, and strengthen materials almost in real time.
When an RFE arrived, the same method continued: quick action, careful review, and consistent follow-up. The goal was to reinforce every element of the case with clear and credible evidence. Through disciplined teamwork and constant coordination, they built a response strong enough to overcome every question raised.

## A Hard-Earned Approval
O ur client’s change of status petition was approved in just over three months . The outcome was especially meaningful given how difficult the case had been. Attorney Dallan Bunce reflected on it candidly, saying, “It ended up being a harder file than expected because of the time constraints. That’s why we were so excited about the result.”
The approval came after the team’s detailed RFE response, which traced every dollar of the investment and proved that the business was real, active, and ready to operate. A strong legal strategy and our client’s persistence and trust in the process helped move fast, close every gap, and meet the strict requirements on time.

## Steering a New Chapter in Freight Logistics
With the approval secured, our client is now focused on expanding his refrigerated freight operations in the United States. His next steps include adding new trucks to the fleet, hiring skilled drivers, and strengthening partnerships with logistics brokers.
After years of managing transport both at sea and on the road, he now channels that same discipline and leadership into building a lasting business in the United States. Each successful delivery moves him closer to his vision of creating a company that keeps essential goods moving safely and efficiently across the country.
“ My goal is always to make the process easier for the client. If that means more communication or going back and forth on drafts, that’s what I’ll do. […] What helped in this case was how engaged the client was. He knew what he wanted to build and was willing to do the work. That kind of clarity makes all the difference […] Sometimes a case turns out to be more complicated than it looks, but if you stay focused and keep communicating, you can still get it across the finish line . ”
